name: Ruta de la Encina — Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — round trip
name_original: Ruta de la Encina — Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — ida y vuelta
region_intro: Jogging routes Los Navalucillos offer access to diverse landscapes within the Montes de Toledo and the Jara Region. The area features extensive Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, bordering the Cabañeros National Park. The Pusa River provides tranquil paths along its banks, while the region also includes the "La Chorrera" waterfall and the challenging Pico Rocigalgo, one of the highest hills in the province. This varied terrain provides a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around Los Navalucillos The most popular jogging route is Chorro de los Navalucillos Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This route leads to the impressive "La Chorrera" waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop from Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, a difficult 11.1 miles (17.9 km) path. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain towards Pico Rocigalgo within Cabañeros National Park. Local runners also love the Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through the Pusa Narrows, often completed in about 32 minutes. Jogging around Los Navalucillos is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and the hills of the Montes de Toledo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Los Navalucillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Los Navalucillos?
A: There are 6 dedicated running routes available around Los Navalucillos on komoot, offering a mix of easy and challenging terrain. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Montes de Toledo and the Jara Region.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Los Navalucillos offers 3 easy running routes perfect for beginners or families. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021436072/boqueron-del-estena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón del Estena Trail&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021486783/malpasillo-bridge-and-pusa-narrows-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering gentle paths through scenic areas.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. For a more strenuous experience, you can tackle routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop from Parque Nacional de Cabañeros&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial elevation gain as it ascends towards Pico Rocigalgo, one of the highest peaks in the Montes de Toledo.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021433391/chorro-de-los-navalucillos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Navalucillos Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the impressive "La Chorrera" waterfall, also known as El Chorro. You can also find the Chorrera Chica Waterfall along the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on the running trails?
A: Jogging routes in Los Navalucillos traverse diverse natural landscapes, including extensive Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, tranquil paths along the Pusa River, and the rugged hills of the Montes de Toledo, bordering the Cabañeros National Park.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021486783/malpasillo-bridge-and-pusa-narrows-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a circular path, as does the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop from Parque Nacional de Cabañeros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while running?
A: Along the routes, you can discover several natural highlights. These include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo&lt;/a&gt; (the highest point in Toledo province), and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant Marine Worm Tracks&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Los Navalucillos?
A: Over 20 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain around Los Navalucillos. They often praise the region's natural beauty, the diversity of the landscapes, and the opportunities for both relaxed riverside runs and challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow a river?
A: Yes, the Pusa River offers tranquil paths along its banks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021486783/malpasillo-bridge-and-pusa-narrows-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through the Pusa Narrows, providing a refreshing riverside running experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Los Navalucillos?
A: The varied topography and Mediterranean climate make Los Navalucillos suitable for jogging across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures, while winter can provide cooler, crisp conditions. Summer mornings and evenings are also popular to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Cabañeros National Park?
A: Given Los Navalucillos' proximity to Cabañeros National Park, many routes offer glimpses or direct access to its characteristic landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is located within the National Park, providing an immersive experience of its natural environment.
tips: Located next to the exit of the jet route which is about 9 km round trip. This one is smaller, although less attractive.

The Holm Oak in Los Navalucillos, within Cabañeros National Park, is the focal point of an interpretive route known as the Centennial Holm Oak Route.
The trail runs through the Arañosa Valley, between rockrose and holm oak trees.
The park's three northern valleys can be seen: Arañosa, Chorro, and Calanchera.
In the flattest part, you can see the peaks of the Sierra Fría, Toledana, and Botija mountains.
In front of an old farmhouse stands the Holm Oak, a large, centuries-old tree, ideal for resting under its shade and admiring its majesty.
